steps.title: Factor the common power
steps.content:
Both terms share $q^{19}$, so factor that out first:
$$
64q^{21}-169q^{19}=q^{19}(64q^2-169)
$$

steps.title: Use difference of squares
steps.content:
The remaining factor is a difference of squares because $64q^2=(8q)^2$ and $169=13^2$:
$$
64q^2-169=(8q)^2-13^2=(8q-13)(8q+13)
$$

steps.title: Match the factor
steps.content: The full factorization is $q^{19}(8q-13)(8q+13)$. Therefore $8q+13$ appears directly as a factor of the original expression. The answer is A.
